questions.question: Which scheduling algorithm may cause starvation?
questions.explanation: In priority scheduling, lower priority processes may never execute if higher priority processes keep arriving, causing starvation.

questions.question: Which layer of the OSI model is responsible for encryption?
questions.explanation: The Presentation layer handles encryption, compression, and data formatting.

questions.question: Which data structure works on FIFO principle?
questions.explanation: Queue follows First In First Out (FIFO) order.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for secure web communication?
questions.explanation: HTTPS encrypts HTTP communication using SSL/TLS.

questions.question: Which memory is volatile?
questions.explanation: RAM loses its contents when power is turned off.

questions.question: Which SQL command is used to remove a table structure completely?
questions.explanation: DROP removes the entire table structure from the database.

questions.question: Which of the following is a NoSQL database?
questions.explanation: MongoDB is a document-based NoSQL database.

questions.question: Which component performs arithmetic and logical operations?
questions.explanation: Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) performs arithmetic and logical operations.

questions.question: Which topology connects all devices to a central hub?
questions.explanation: In star topology, all nodes connect to a central hub.

questions.question: Which port does HTTPS use by default?
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses port 443 for secure communication.

questions.question: Which key is used to uniquely identify a record in a database table?
questions.explanation: A primary key uniquely identifies each record in a table.

questions.question: Which algorithm is used for public-key encryption?
questions.explanation: RSA is a widely used public-key cryptography algorithm.

questions.question: Which layer of TCP/IP corresponds to the OSI Transport layer?
questions.explanation: The TCP/IP Transport layer performs the same function as the OSI Transport layer.

questions.question: Which device forwards packets between networks?
questions.explanation: Routers forward packets between different networks.

questions.question: Which normal form removes transitive dependency?
questions.explanation: Third Normal Form removes transitive dependencies.

questions.question: Which command is used to create a table in SQL?
questions.explanation: CREATE TABLE is used to create a table structure.

questions.question: Which sorting algorithm has the best average-case time complexity?
questions.explanation: Merge sort has O(n log n) average time complexity.

questions.question: Which HTTP method is used to retrieve data from a server?
questions.explanation: GET is used to retrieve data from a server.

questions.question: Which type of malware replicates itself automatically?
questions.explanation: Worms replicate themselves across networks without user intervention.

questions.question: Which file system is commonly used in Linux?
questions.explanation: EXT4 is a widely used Linux file system.

questions.question: Which device operates at Layer 2 of the OSI model?
questions.explanation: Switches operate at the Data Link layer.

questions.question: Which protocol is used for sending email?
questions.explanation: SMTP is used for sending email.

questions.question: Which DNS record maps a domain name to an IPv4 address?
questions.explanation: A records map domain names to IPv4 addresses.

questions.question: Which memory is closest to the CPU?
questions.explanation: Cache memory is located closest to the CPU for fast access.

questions.question: Which programming paradigm does Java primarily follow?
questions.explanation: Java is primarily an object-oriented programming language.

questions.question: Which protocol assigns IP addresses automatically?
questions.explanation: DHCP dynamically assigns IP addresses to devices.

questions.question: Which hashing algorithm is considered insecure today?
questions.explanation: MD5 is vulnerable to collision attacks and is no longer considered secure.

questions.question: Which Linux command displays the current directory?
questions.explanation: pwd stands for Print Working Directory.

questions.question: Which cloud service model provides virtual machines?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure as a Service provides virtual machines and infrastructure.

questions.question: Which algorithm is commonly used in blockchain mining?
questions.explanation: Bitcoin mining uses SHA-256 hashing.
